mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, policy easing lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside simultaneous macro deterioration that overwhelms the discount-rate effect.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are negative in most falling-FEDFUNDS windows or if the sign hit rate drops below 45% in any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL above prior trend raises expected policy tightening and compresses real equity valuations, so subsequent S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days should be negative. The effect is strongest when inflation surprises are broad rather than transitory.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month average by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the negative average return disappears after conditioning on higher FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income growth and softer future demand, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises for idiosyncratic labor-force reasons without broader demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are positive in a majority of UNRATE jump episodes or if the signal's rolling hit rate stays below 40% for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail spending, which should lift near-term S&P 500 returns as demand-sensitive earnings expectations improve. The mechanism weakens if sales rise only because of price inflation rather than real volume demand.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone, or if the next-5-day return is negative in more than 60% of joint-improvement observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and operating leverage, which should support equity prices over the next 20 trading days through better cyclical earnings expectations. The signal fails when output gains come from inventory rebuilding without final-demand confirmation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO turnarounds are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect vanishes after one quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDPC1 growth shock combined with rising RSAFS indicates genuine final-demand acceleration, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is falsified when GDP growth improves but retail sales stall, implying the growth impulse is not broad-based.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth exceeds 2% and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-growth condition does not deliver positive 60-day returns with at least 55% hit rate, or if GDPC1 alone explains the result better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS indicates a synchronous slowdown in employment and income, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when both series weaken in the same month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the same 1-month window
failure_condition: Reject if same-month UNRATE/PAYEMS deterioration is followed by positive 20-day returns in most cases, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is stable or falling while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real financing conditions stay easy and equity duration exposure is rewarded, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days. The signal fails if low rates coexist with an inflation breakout that forces market repricing of future tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 2-month change is nonpositive
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate/low-inflation condition does not improve 5-day hit rate above 55%, or if returns are negative on average despite the trigger.
